methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

data_flows.source: User (msg.sender) providing GTC tokens and duration
path: Validate unlockTime in [12w, 104w] range and >= existing unlockTime
transformations: uint88 amount added to existing stake
uint64 duration added to block.timestamp for unlockTime
data_flows.trust_changes: User tokens move from user-controlled to contract-controlled with time lock
exposure_risks: Post-lock period: stake remains slashable until withdrawn
manipulation_risks: No protection against dust staking (very small amounts immune to slashing due to integer division)

transformations: amount added to community stake struct
data_flows.trust_changes: User tokens locked under both staker and stakee's reputation risk
exposure_risks: Stakee misbehavior leads to staker losing funds through cascading slash
manipulation_risks: Staker cannot control when stakee misbehaves; post-lock slashing risk

data_flows.source: SLASHER_ROLE authorized address submitting slash arrays and percent
path: For each selfStaker: compute slashedAmount = (percent * stake.amount) / 100
Handle round transitions: move from round N-1 to N, or zero stale slashes
data_flows.trust_changes: Tokens move from active stake to slashed state; still in contract but pending burn
exposure_risks: SLASHER_ROLE is fully trusted; can slash any stake at any percent
Duplicate addresses in arrays cause compounding slashes
manipulation_risks: Truncation to zero for small stakes: (1 * 99) / 100 = 0
Slashing zero-amount stakes still updates slashedInRound affecting future release logic

data_flows.source: RELEASER_ROLE restoring slashed funds after successful appeal
path: If staker == stakee: restore to selfStake, else communityStake
data_flows.trust_changes: Tokens return from slashed state to active stake
exposure_risks: CRITICAL: userTotalStaked is NOT updated on release - creates permanent accounting mismatch
manipulation_risks: Released funds become unwithdrawable because userTotalStaked underflows on withdraw

data_flows.source: Any caller (permissionless) triggering round progression and burn
data_flows.trust_changes: Slashed tokens become permanently burned; exit the system
exposure_risks: totalSlashed[roundToBurn] is NOT zeroed after burn, leaving stale data
manipulation_risks: Permissionless call: anyone can trigger burn as soon as minimum duration passes
If burnAddress is misconfigured, tokens could go to wrong destination

data_flows.trust_changes: Tokens move from contract-controlled back to user-controlled
manipulation_risks: If userTotalStaked is desynchronized (from release bug), withdrawal reverts due to underflow

core_rules: Stakes are locked for 12-104 weeks and can only be extended, not shortened
Self-stakes represent personal reputation; community stakes vouch for others
All staked amounts (locked and unlocked-but-not-withdrawn) are slashable
Slashing is percentage-based (1-100%) and executed by SLASHER_ROLE
Appeal window is enforced by burnRoundMinimumDuration (90 days) between burns
RELEASER_ROLE can restore slashed amounts before burn occurs
lockAndBurn is permissionless but rate-limited by burnRoundMinimumDuration
userTotalStaked should equal sum of all stake.amounts for a user across self and community stakes
economic_incentives: Stakers are incentivized to stake on trustworthy identities to avoid slashing losses
Longer lock periods signal higher commitment (12-104 weeks)
Slashing creates economic penalty for Sybil or malicious behavior
Community staking creates skin-in-the-game for vouching
Post-lock vulnerability incentivizes prompt withdrawal of unlocked stakes
governance_mechanisms: DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE controls upgrade authority, role management, and can grant/revoke SLASHER/RELEASER/PAUSER roles
SLASHER_ROLE has unilateral power to slash any stake at any percentage - centralization risk
RELEASER_ROLE can restore slashed funds before burn - appeal mechanism
PAUSER_ROLE can halt all operations via pause/unpause
No timelock on admin actions (upgrades, role changes)
No on-chain governance or multisig requirement for slashing decisions

edge_cases.scenario: Release function does not update userTotalStaked
edge_cases.expected_behavior: After release, userTotalStaked should be incremented by amountToRelease to stay consistent with actual stake amounts
edge_cases.actual_behavior: userTotalStaked remains at the post-slash reduced value while stake.amount is restored, creating a permanent desynchronization
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: After a slash-then-release cycle, the user cannot withdraw the released amount because userTotalStaked -= amount in withdrawSelfStake/withdrawCommunityStake will underflow and revert. Funds are permanently locked in the contract. Example: stake 100, slash 50 (userTotalStaked=50), release 50 (userTotalStaked still 50, amount=100). Withdraw 100 fails because 50-100 underflows. User can only withdraw 50, losing 50 tokens permanently.

edge_cases.scenario: Slash produces zero slashedAmount due to integer truncation
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Either skip the slash for that stake or ensure minimum 1 unit is slashed
edge_cases.actual_behavior: slashedAmount = 0 but slashedInRound is still updated to currentSlashRound, and slashedAmount += 0. This corrupts the slash tracking metadata without actually slashing anything.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A user with a tiny stake (e.g. 99 wei) cannot be effectively slashed at low percentages. The slashedInRound update could interfere with future legitimate slashes if the round changes (the stale slash handling may zero out a previous legitimate slashedAmount).

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Each address slashed once at the specified percent
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Each occurrence slashes the remaining amount again, compounding the effect. Two 50% slashes result in 75% total slash, not 50%.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Malicious or negligent SLASHER_ROLE could include duplicate addresses to slash more than intended percent

edge_cases.expected_behavior: totalSlashed mapping should be zeroed after burning to prevent stale data
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Mapping retains stale value after tokens are transferred. While this doesn't cause direct financial loss (the stale value is never used for future operations), it creates inaccurate on-chain accounting data.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Off-chain systems reading totalSlashed for burned rounds get incorrect data. No direct on-chain exploit.

edge_cases.actual_behavior: Succeeds, emitting a SelfStakeWithdrawn event with amount 0 and executing a zero-value token transfer
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Gas griefing, misleading events. No financial impact.

edge_cases.scenario: Staker slashed in round N-2 or earlier, then slashed again - stale slash handling
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Old slashedAmount from already-burned rounds should be properly handled
edge_cases.actual_behavior: sStake.slashedAmount is set to 0 (correct, since those funds were burned). But the already-burned totalSlashed mapping for the old round still contains the stale value (not decremented). This is inconsistent but doesn't cause loss because the old round's totalSlashed is never used again.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No direct exploit. The stale totalSlashed for old rounds is cosmetic.

edge_cases.scenario: currentSlashRound = 1 and release called with slashRound = 0
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should be rejected since round 0 is a non-standard round
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Passes the RoundAlreadyBurned check (0 < 0 is false). Would then check slashedInRound == 0, which could match an unslashed stake. However, amountToRelease > slashedAmount (which would be 0 for unslashed) would catch any positive amount.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A release(staker, staker, 0, 0) call on an unslashed stake would succeed as a no-op. No financial impact but could emit confusing events if events were emitted (they aren't in release).

edge_cases.scenario: burnAddress set to address(0) or incorrect address during initialization
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Burned tokens should be permanently removed from circulation
edge_cases.actual_behavior: No validation of burnAddress in initialize. If set to address(0) and GTC reverts on zero-address transfers, lockAndBurn would permanently revert when there are slashed tokens, bricking the burn mechanism.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Misconfiguration could prevent burns, causing slashed funds to remain unreleased and new rounds unable to progress. The contract would need an upgrade to fix.

edge_cases.scenario: Slash called on a stake that was just released in the current round
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Slash should apply to the restored amount
edge_cases.actual_behavior: After release, stake.amount is restored and stake.slashedAmount is reduced. If slashed again in the same round, the round check passes (slashedInRound == currentSlashRound), and new slashedAmount is calculated on the full restored amount. However, the old remaining slashedAmount is preserved and the new slash is added on top. This is correct behavior.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No exploit, but complex interactions between slash and release in the same round should be carefully managed by operators.

assumption_violations: CRITICAL: The invariant 'userTotalStaked[addr] == sum of all stake.amounts for addr' is violated after any release operation
Assumption that totalSlashed accurately reflects contract's token obligations breaks after lockAndBurn (stale values remain)
Assumption that SLASHER_ROLE always provides deduplicated address arrays is not enforced on-chain
Assumption that GTC token behaves as standard ERC20 (returns bool, doesn't have fee-on-transfer) - not using SafeERC20
Assumption that burnAddress is valid and can receive tokens is not validated at initialization
Missing storage gap (__gap) for safe upgradability - new state variables in upgrades could collide with storage of child contracts
game_theory_risks: SLASHER_ROLE has unchecked unilateral power - no on-chain checks on slash correctness, no timelock, no multisig requirement
Post-lock period creates a race condition: users must withdraw immediately after unlock or risk being slashed on unlocked funds
Community stakers bear disproportionate risk - their funds can be slashed due to stakee misbehavior they cannot control or predict
Permissionless lockAndBurn allows any party to trigger burn as soon as minimum duration passes, potentially before off-chain appeal processes complete
No mechanism for partial appeal - RELEASER_ROLE must know exact slash amounts per stake to release correctly
Slashing at 100% combined with short appeal period (if burnRoundMinimumDuration is changed) could enable griefing
integration_risks: If GTC token is upgraded or replaced, the contract would need upgrade to update token reference
No SafeERC20 usage - tokens that don't return bool on transfer would cause issues
UUPS proxy pattern - if _authorizeUpgrade is compromised, entire contract including funds is at risk
Missing __UUPSUpgradeable_init() call in initializer (may be benign depending on OZ version)
No reentrancy guard - relies on checks-effects-interactions pattern which is correctly followed but fragile if modified in upgrade
calculation_errors: CRITICAL: release() fails to execute userTotalStaked[staker] += amountToRelease, causing permanent accounting desynchronization and fund lockup
Integer truncation in slash: (percent * amount) / 100 rounds down, allowing micro-stakes to avoid slashing entirely
No rounding compensation: repeated small percentage slashes compound truncation errors, resulting in slightly less total slashed than expected

token_semantics.notes: Test GTC contract uses uint96 balances (max ~79 billion tokens w/ 18 decimals). Production GTC has standard ERC20 behavior. Contract uses IERC20 without SafeERC20 wrapper. transferFrom in test contract has no allowance check (test only). Cannot transfer to address(0).

invariants.name: userTotalStaked_consistency
invariants.description: userTotalStaked[addr] should equal selfStakes[addr].amount + sum of all communityStakes[addr][stakee].amount for all stakees. VIOLATED after release() operations.

invariants.name: totalSlashed_token_balance_consistency
invariants.description: Contract token balance should equal sum of all stake.amount values plus sum of totalSlashed for unburned rounds. Partially violated because totalSlashed is not zeroed after burn.

invariants.name: slash_round_monotonicity
invariants.description: currentSlashRound only increases (via lockAndBurn). Initialized at 1, incremented by 1 each burn cycle.

invariants.name: unlock_time_monotonicity
invariants.description: Lock periods can only be extended, never shortened for a given stake.

invariants.name: stake_amount_conservation
invariants.description: When slashing: stake.amount decreases by X, stake.slashedAmount increases by X, totalSlashed increases by X. When releasing: stake.slashedAmount decreases by X, stake.amount increases by X, totalSlashed decreases by X. Conservation should hold within active rounds.
